name: Chocolate Fudge--And Other Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9 inch square or 13x9 inch pan with foil, extending over the sides of the pan; butter the foil.
2. In a large saucepan, combine sugar, margarine, and evaporated milk. Heat to boiling over medium heat, stirring constantly.
3. Continue boiling for 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
4. Add marshmallow creme and chocolate chips; blend until smooth. Stir in walnuts and vanilla.
5. Pour the mixture into the buttered, foil-lined pan. Allow it to cool to room temperature.
6. Once cooled, score the fudge into 36-48 squares. Refrigerate until firm.
7. Remove the fudge from the pan by lifting the foil. Carefully remove the foil from the sides of the fudge.
8. Using a large knife, cut through the scored lines. Store in the refrigerator.
Makes about 3 pounds--36-48 squares.
Variations:
Butterscotch Fudge: Substitute 1 12 oz package of butterscotch chips for chocolate chips and pecans for walnuts.
Confetti Fudge: Substitute 2 cups candy-coated chocolate pieces for walnuts. Stir 1 1/2 cups of the chocolate pieces into the fudge with vanilla. Pour into buttered, foil-lined pan. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup chocolate pieces over top and press lightly into warm fudge. Cool to room temperature. Do not refrigerate before cutting. Store in refrigerator; let stand at room temperature before serving.
Turtle Fudge: Substitute cashews for walnuts and add 24 quartered caramels with cashews and vanilla. Cool to room temperature. Do not refrigerate before cutting. Store in refrigerator; let stand at room temperature before serving.
